top_signals.title: China’s Oil Buying Pause Won’t Last Forever
top_signals.summary: China has been cited as an example of a country that has managed to insulate itself relatively well against oil crises. With over a billion barrels in estimated inventories before the war in the Middle East started, China was the poster child of forward planning in energy security. But this could change, and if it does, it would make an already severe crisis even worse. Kpler sounded the alarm about that prospect earlier this month, reporting that Chinese refiners have reduced their purchases of oil from overseas because of the price surge caused…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Hegseth says US-China ties are ‘better than in years’
top_signals.summary: Pentagon chief still presses allies to boost defence spending to counter ‘military build-up’ by Beijing

top_signals.title: Mistral to explore designing own chips, CEO says, as it ramps up infrastructure build
top_signals.summary: Mistral's semiconductor ambitions underscore the French startup's bid to control more of its infrastructure as it competes with OpenAI and Anthropic.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Experts urge Japan to use pictorial warnings on tobacco packages
top_signals.summary: Many countries require cigarette packages to display images warning of health hazards related to smoking, such as a blackened lung.

top_signals.title: Manchester City and Spygate prove lawyer gulf is opening in football
top_signals.summary: In February 2023, Manchester City fans unfurled a banner at the Etihad Stadium bearing the words “Pannick on the streets of London”. This play on The Smiths’ 1986 song referred to Lord David Pannick KC, the barrister hired to spearhead the club’s defence against 115 charges for alleged breaches of the Premier League’s financial rules. [...]
